title: Gently Buttery Chinese Naan Bread Flatbread
description: A buttery and light Chinese dish featuring naan bread, perfect for a nostalgic treat, this recipe is also sugar-free-friendly. It combines the flavors of fry-grind-rice-khao-khua with the softness of naan bread.
instructions: Preheat a non-stick skillet or griddle over medium heat.
Butter one side of each naan bread piece with the softened unsalted butter.
Place the buttered side of the naan bread down on the preheated skillet.
Sprinkle a pinch of salt, black pepper, and garlic powder over the naan bread.
Cook the naan bread for 2-3 minutes or until it is golden brown and crispy.
Flip the naan bread over and cook for an additional 1-2 minutes.
While the naan bread is cooking, mix the fry-grind-rice-khao-khua with chopped green onions and a drizzle of sesame oil.
Once the naan bread is cooked, let it cool for a minute before serving with the fry-grind-rice-khao-khua mixture.
